description: GSDMD blockade EC50 anchors (cellular + cell-free) and NLRP3-pathway EC50 anchors for comparison. The ratio between cellular GSDMD-EC50 (Hu 2020) and cellular NLRP3-pathway-EC50 (Xu 2024) is load-bearing for the dose-window analysis.

primary_target.name: GSDMD pore formation (CP6b, pyroptotic exit)
primary_target.mechanism: Covalent modification of Cys191 on N-terminal GSDMD fragment, blocking oligomerization into membrane pores. Caspase-1-mediated GSDMD cleavage proceeds normally; only the pore-formation step is blocked.
primary_target.cell_free_IC50_source: Hu 2020 Nat Immunol, PMID 32367036, PMC7316630. Fluorogenic Tb3+ liposome leakage assay. IC50 = 0.3 μM for inhibiting wildtype GSDMD pore formation; 0.04-0.07 μM for inhibiting full-length GSDMD + caspase-11. C191A mutant IC50 ~8-fold higher (~2.4 μM), confirming Cys191 covalent mechanism.
primary_target.cellular_IC50_source: Hu 2020 — preincubation IC50 for inhibiting cellular pyroptosis decreased 24-fold to ~0.02 μM (20 nM) after 1 hr pre-treatment, reflecting covalent target engagement kinetics. Naive single-addition cellular IC50 ~0.5 μM (estimated from inhibition curves).

secondary_target_upstream.name: NLRP3 inflammasome priming + assembly (CP2)
secondary_target_upstream.mechanism: Disulfiram covalently modifies NLRP3 Cys126, preventing palmitoylation by zDHHC5/zDHHC7 and disrupting trans-Golgi-network localization. Upstream of GSDMD. Blocks ASC speck formation, caspase-1 maturation, IL-1β release.
secondary_target_upstream.source: Xu, Pickard, Núñez 2024 Cell Rep, PMC11398858. LPS-primed BMDMs + ATP/nigericin/imiquimod stimulation, caspase-1 p20 + GSDMD cleavage + IL-1β ELISA readout. 10 μM partial inhibition; 30-50 μM complete inhibition; below 5 μM no observable NLRP3 effect.
secondary_target_upstream.in_vivo_outcome: Significant reduction in serum + peritoneal IL-1β in LPS, Salmonella, and MSU peritonitis models

in_vivo_gout_rat.model: Potassium oxonate (250 mg/kg/d × 21d, IP) → MSU crystal sub-plantar injection → 9-day disulfiram treatment
in_vivo_gout_rat.outcomes: Significant ↓paw swelling (p<0.05), ↓inflammation index (p<0.001), ↓serum urate (p<0.001), ↓IL-1β/TNF-α/IL-6 vs disease control. Combination DSF + allopurinol (10 mg/kg) > either monotherapy.
in_vivo_gout_rat.limitations_acknowledged_in_paper: Single dose tested; no PK measurements; no dose-response; no XO or transporter assays; long-term safety not assessed.
in_vivo_gout_rat.HED_method: Standard FDA allometric scaling, rat-to-human factor /6.2: 50 mg/kg ÷ 6.2 = 8.06 mg/kg × 60 kg = 484 mg. Within typical AUD dosing range (250-500 mg).

comparative_ranking_of_concentration_thr.note: GSDMD blockade requires plasma concentrations 50-1,500-fold lower than NLRP3-upstream blockade. This is the key opportunity for sub-AUD dosing: if 100-250 mg PO achieves plasma DSF ≥ 0.3 μM (which clinical PK suggests it does), GSDMD is engaged; NLRP3-palmitoylation pathway (much higher EC50) is NOT engaged at these doses; this leaves the GSDMD-distal effect dominant without simultaneous suppression of upstream inflammasome assembly (which has its own immunological cost-benefit profile).
